description: Known-public scope allowlist per ecosystem. Bundled as a reasoning aid for DISCOVER — a scoped dependency whose scope appears here (e.g. npm `@aws-sdk/*`, `@types/*`, `@anthropic-ai/*`) is widely public, LM-familiar, and unlikely to be worth a separate reference file. The model may consult or ignore this list per its judgment under the goal-given DISCOVER posture. Bundled at plugin release time; updated manually as new authoritative public scopes surface. Updates ship in plugin MINOR releases per the version-evolution doctrine (docs/10-version-evolution.md). The `last_updated` field is the staleness signal — when more than ~6 months old, the list may not reflect newer authoritative scopes; bump the plugin to refresh.

npm.description: npm scoped packages (@scope/pkg). Scopes here are well-known public publishers; their packages are LM-familiar and not worth proposing as companion sources.

python.description: Python doesn't have lexical scopes the way npm does, but PyPI publishers often share a recognizable prefix. Treat package-name prefixes here as scope-like; a bare name starting with one of these prefixes is COMMODITY.

rust.description: Cargo doesn't use scopes; this list is reserved for future use if community scope conventions emerge.

go.description: Go modules use full import paths; treat path-prefix matches as scope-like. A bare-name dep whose module path starts with one of these prefixes is COMMODITY.

jvm.description: Maven groupIds. A dependency whose groupId matches one of these is COMMODITY.

php.description: Composer vendor names. A package matching `<vendor>/<name>` where vendor appears here is COMMODITY.

ruby.description: RubyGems doesn't have scopes; this list is reserved for future use if community prefix conventions emerge.

github-actions.description: GitHub Actions `uses:` lines reference org/repo paths. Workflows that `uses: actions/checkout@v4` reference the well-known public `actions/` org — COMMODITY. Workflows that reference a private internal org are INTEGRAL.
